# instanode.dev

> Zero-setup infrastructure. One HTTP call = real database.

## API Base URL

https://api.instanode.dev

## Quick start

Provision a Postgres database (no account, no API key):
```
curl -s -X POST https://api.instanode.dev/db/new | jq
```

Provision a Redis cache:
```
curl -s -X POST https://api.instanode.dev/cache/new | jq
```

Provision a webhook receiver:
```
curl -s -X POST https://api.instanode.dev/webhook/new | jq
```

## Endpoints

### POST /db/new
Provision a Postgres database with pgvector.
Returns `connection_url` (standard postgres:// URL), `token`, `tier`, `limits`.
Anonymous tier: 10MB storage, 2 connections, expires in 24h.

### POST /cache/new
Provision a Redis cache with ACL namespace isolation.
Returns `connection_url` (standard redis:// URL), `key_prefix`, `token`, `tier`, `limits`.
Anonymous tier: 5MB memory, expires in 24h.

### POST /webhook/new
Provision a webhook receiver.
Returns `receive_url`, `token`, `tier`, `limits`, `expires_at`.
POST any payload to `receive_url` to store it. Anonymous: 100 stored, expires 24h.

### POST /webhook/receive/{token}
Send a webhook payload. Accepts any Content-Type. Stores headers + body in Redis.
Returns `{ "ok": true, "id": "<request-id>" }`.

### GET /healthz
Returns `{ "ok": true, "service": "instant-lite" }`.

## Response shape (all provisioning endpoints)

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "id": "uuid",
  "token": "uuid",
  "connection_url": "postgres://...",
  "tier": "anonymous",
  "limits": { "storage_mb": 10, "connections": 2, "expires_in": "24h" },
  "note": "Works now. Keep it forever (free 14-day trial): https://..."
}
```

## Tiers

| Tier | Price | Postgres | Redis | Webhooks | Expiry |
|------|-------|----------|-------|----------|--------|
| Free | $0 | 10MB / 2 conn | 5MB | 100 stored | 24h |
| Developer | $12/mo | 500MB / 5 conn | 25MB | 1000 stored | Never |

## No authentication required

All provisioning endpoints work without any headers or API keys.
Anonymous resources expire in 24h. The response `note` field contains a URL
to claim resources permanently with a free account.

## Standard protocols

All connection URLs use standard protocol schemes:
- Postgres: `postgres://user:pass@host:port/dbname`
- Redis: `redis://user:pass@host:port/db`

Works with every standard driver. No wrapper SDK needed.
